heading: Effect of subsequent employment with the Government
label: 3524 Effect of subsequent employment with the Government
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “Postal Regulatory Commission” for “Postal Rate Commission” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 109–4352004—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “Government Accountability Office” for “General Accounting Office” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 108–271
Effective DateSection effective 60 days after , see , set out as a note under .Nov. 25, 2002section 1313(a)(4) of Pub. L. 107–296section 3521 of this title
amendments: Amendments2006—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “Postal Regulatory Commission” for “Postal Rate Commission” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 109–4352004—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “Government Accountability Office” for “General Accounting Office” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 108–271

children.text: in subsection (b) includes employment under a personal services contract (or other direct contract) with the United States Government (other than an entity in the legislative branch); and
in subsection (c) does not include employment under such a contract.

children.label: b
children.text: An individual who has received a voluntary separation incentive payment under this subchapter and accepts any employment for compensation with the Government of the United States with 5 years after the date of the separation on which the payment is based shall be required to pay, before the individual’s first day of employment, the entire amount of the incentive payment to the agency that paid the incentive payment.1So in original. Probably should be “within”.1

children.label: A
children.text: the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position; or

children.label: i
children.text: has skills directly related to resolving the emergency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will serve on a temporary basis only so long as that individual’s services are made necessary by the emergency.

If the employment under this section is with an entity in the legislative branch, the head of the entity or the appointing official may waive the repayment if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.

If the employment under this section is with the judicial branch, the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may waive the repayment if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.
